entry_requirements.asvab_line_score: GT (General Technical) >= 110 and ST (Skilled Technical) >= 112. Cross-corroborated across secondary sources (battalionduty.com, military-ranks.org); NOT independently confirmed against a primary Army document (DA Pam 611-21) this session.
entry_requirements.education: High school diploma or GED, standard Army enlistment policy; not independently confirmed as having a 17C-specific diploma-only requirement this session.
entry_requirements.age_range: 17-35 (general Army active-duty enlistment age policy; not independently re-verified this session).
entry_requirements.physical: Standard Army Combat Fitness Test (ACFT) and medical accession standards apply; no 17C-specific physical standard. Day-to-day duty is console/watch-floor based once MOS-qualified.
entry_requirements.security_clearance: Top Secret/SCI (TS/SCI); must be able to pass a Single Scope Background Investigation (SSBI). This is a hard gate independent of ASVAB score: per SEAD 4 (the government-wide National Security Adjudicative Guidelines), common disqualifiers include foreign influence/preference (close foreign-national contacts, dual citizenship, foreign-government ties), financial considerations (unresolved delinquent debt, bankruptcy pattern, tax delinquency), and drug involvement/substance misuse (recent illegal drug use or a pattern of misuse) - a qualifying ASVAB/GT score does not offset a disqualifying finding under these guidelines.
entry_requirements.other: U.S. citizenship required for TS/SCI eligibility. Reported as a highly competitive, low-availability MOS given the combined ASVAB threshold and clearance bar.

training_pipeline.description: Standard Army entry-level soldiering, fitness, and marksmanship training common to all Army enlistees.

training_pipeline.location: Corry Station, FL (joint-service Center for Information Warfare Training annex)
training_pipeline.description: Foundational cyber, networking, and IT coursework at a joint-service schoolhouse shared with Navy and Marine Corps cyber students.
Advanced cyber operations coursework at the Army Cyber Center of Excellence.

pay_and_compensation.methodology_note: Computed, not authoritative - E-1 (<2yr) basic pay + BAS, excluding BAH (duty-station-specific; never a single national figure per data/pay-tables.json) and excluding quantified non-cash value (TSP match, TRICARE). Actual total compensation is materially higher once a sourced duty-station BAH figure is added.
pay_and_compensation.source: data/pay-tables.json (this project's COMPENSATION output), ultimately DFAS 2026 Military Pay Tables per FY2026 NDAA

career_progression.typical_promotion_timeline: General Army enlisted timeline, not 17C-specific and not independently re-verified this session: E-1 to E-4 in approximately 2-3 years via time-in-service/time-in-grade; E-5 promotion-board eligibility typically follows around 4-6 years.
reenlistment_decision_points: End of initial enlistment contract (cyber contracts commonly run longer than average given training investment - not independently confirmed this session)
Continuation pay election window (year 7-12 per Army's published schedule; see retirement block)
Selective Retention Bonus (SRB) eligibility windows - the Army has publicly reported extending SRBs specifically to retain cyber-qualified soldiers
advanced_schools_or_certs: Advanced cyber operations coursework at the Fort Eisenhower Cyber Center of Excellence
DoD 8140-aligned vendor certifications funded via Army Credentialing Assistance/COOL (e.g., CompTIA Security+, CySA+)
Joint Cyber Analysis Course (JCAC) follow-on track for some assignments
career_progression.officer_conversion_path: Commission into the Army Cyber Branch (17A, Cyber Operations Officer) via Green-to-Gold, OCS, or direct commission for qualified enlisted soldiers; specific eligibility criteria not independently verified this session.
career_progression.source: Army Selective Retention Bonus reporting (army.mil/article/149561); general Army promotion-policy knowledge, flagged as directional and not independently re-verified this session

retirement.twenty_year_cliff_note: The defined-benefit pension is all-or-nothing at 20 years of service; separating one day short yields $0 pension. TSP savings (member's own contributions plus DoD match, vested immediately; DoD's automatic 1% vested after 2 years) are the only guaranteed retirement money before 20 years and roll into a civilian 401(k)/IRA on separation.
retirement.continuation_pay_note: Army's published window runs between the member's 7th and 12th year of service. Statutory range is 2.5x-13x monthly basic pay for active-component members; Army's published active-component rate is 2.5x, in exchange for a minimum 3-year additional service obligation (Army specifically requires 4 years). Whether other services use the same year-7 start was not independently verified.
retirement.guard_reserve_points_note: NOT interchangeable with active-duty retirement. Guard/Reserve uses a points system (1 pt/drill period, 1 pt/active-duty day, up to 15 membership points/year) toward a 20-'good year' (50+ points/year) threshold, with pension starting at age 60, reducible to as low as 50 under a FY2008 NDAA active-duty-mobilization credit.
retirement.lump_sum_option_note: At 20+ years, a retiree may elect a 25% or 50% lump-sum of the discounted present value of future retired pay, with correspondingly reduced monthly pay until Social Security Full Retirement Age (~67); the lump sum is fully taxable in the year received.

transition.skillbridge_notes: DoD-wide SkillBridge: up to 180 days of permissive duty during the final 180 days on active duty, with full pay/benefits continuing (employer does not pay the member). The real gate is unit commander approval, which can be withheld based on unit mission/staffing needs even for an otherwise-eligible member.
transition.tap_program_summary: Mandatory DoD-wide Transition Assistance Program; must begin no later than 365 days before separation. Includes pre-separation counseling, a VA Benefits I course, the DOL Employment Fundamentals of Career Transition workshop, and a Capstone review confirming Career Readiness Standards before separation.
transition.dd214_significance: Governs eligibility for VA disability compensation, VA healthcare enrollment/priority group, GI Bill/education benefits, VA home loan Certificate of Eligibility, federal hiring preference, and state veteran benefits. Block 24 (character of service) gates most benefits before any other criterion.
va_disability_common_claims: Musculoskeletal strain (cervical/lumbar) from sustained console/desk-based watch work - reasoned inference by this session, NOT drawn from a cyber-specific DoD/VA claims dataset; data/transition.json's specialty-linked claims list does not include a cyber entry. Directional only.
Sleep-disorder / circadian-rhythm-linked conditions from rotating shift or watch-floor schedules common to cyber operations centers - same inference caveat.
Mental health conditions (e.g., anxiety, adjustment disorder) - same inference caveat.

PACT Act presumptive conditions (respiratory conditions, certain cancers) IF deployed to a covered CENTCOM/burn-pit-exposure theater - this is a broad, not cyber-specific, presumptive category per data/transition.json.
transition.vr_e_eligibility_note: Chapter 31 (VR&E) requires a 20%+ service-connected disability rating creating an employment handicap, generally filed within a 12-year window from separation/first rating. Funds tuition/training plus a monthly subsistence allowance (FY2026 maximum $3,439.23/month) from an entitlement bucket separate from the GI Bill.

civilian_crosswalk.credential_gap_note: Operational cyber experience and an active clearance do not by themselves confer a civilian certification. Vendor certifications required as a baseline for many civilian/DoD-contractor cyber roles under DoD 8140-equivalent workforce requirements must be separately scheduled and passed as proctored exams - demonstrated operational skill does not waive the exam. The military does not hand you the certification automatically: Army Credentialing Assistance/COOL (see career_progression.advanced_schools_or_certs and education_benefits.credentialing_assistance above) funds the exam fee while still serving, but the soldier must still schedule, sit, and pass the exam independently.
civilian_crosswalk.source: BLS Occupational Outlook Handbook, Information Security Analysts (bls.gov/ooh/computer-and-information-technology/information-security-analysts.htm), median $124,910/yr, 10th-90th percentile range $69,660-$186,420, May 2024 data. O*NET code is moderate-confidence trained knowledge, not independently re-fetched from onetonline.org this session - verify before publishing. Credential-gap framing per data/transition.json.

veteran_benefits.gi_bill_tier_note: Post-9/11 GI Bill (Ch. 33) eligibility scales with cumulative qualifying active-duty days: 100% at 36+ months (or Purple Heart / service-connected-disability discharge after 30+ days); 90% at 30-35 months; 80% at 24-29; 70% at 18-23; 60% at 6-17; 50% at 90 days-5 months. No tier below 50%.
veteran_benefits.gi_bill_transferability_note: Transferable to spouse/dependent children (up to 36 months combined) only while still serving; requires 6+ years of service and a new 4-year additional service obligation (waived for Purple Heart recipients). Dependent children must wait until the member reaches 10 years of service before they can begin using it.
veteran_benefits.va_home_loan_note: Veterans with full entitlement face no VA-imposed loan limit (lender underwriting still applies); no down payment or PMI required for a loan at/below appraised value. A funding fee (1.25%-3.3% depending on down payment and prior use) applies unless the veteran draws VA service-connected disability compensation.
veteran_benefits.va_healthcare_priority_group_note: Priority Group (1-8) depends on VA disability rating, combat-service era, income, and other factors; Groups 1-3 and combat-era enhanced-eligibility veterans (10-year window from discharge for post-11/11/1998 combat service) generally owe no copays.
veteran_benefits.state_benefit_variance_note: State veteran benefits (property tax exemptions, income tax treatment, tuition waivers) are set by individual state legislatures and are NOT a single national figure - e.g., a 100%-disabled veteran receives a full homestead property tax exemption in Texas but a flat $100,000 assessed-value exemption in California. Verify by state of legal residence.

education_benefits.gi_bill_summary: Post-9/11 GI Bill (Ch. 33): full net tuition/fees with no cap at public schools (in-state rate applies regardless of residency); up to $29,920.95/yr (2025-2026 AY) at private/foreign schools; Monthly Housing Allowance keyed to school ZIP code and enrollment tier; $1,000/yr book stipend.
education_benefits.tuition_assistance: DoD-wide standard: $250/semester-hour, capped at $4,500/fiscal year, usable while still serving. Army-specific usage limits (reported 16 semester-hours/fiscal year) and 2025-2026 rule tightening are search-sourced, not independently primary-confirmed this session.
education_benefits.credentialing_assistance: Army Credentialing Assistance (COOL): $2,000/fiscal-year cap (reduced from $4,000 in 2024); reported lifetime limit of 3 credentials per 10 years of service; commissioned officers reported ineligible.
education_benefits.coop_or_apprenticeship_notes: No 17C-specific apprenticeship/co-op program was independently identified this session; standard COOL/Credentialing Assistance funding applies for vendor certifications.

deployment_and_lifestyle.typical_tempo: Primarily garrison/CONUS console and watch-floor work at cyber protection teams, network operations centers, or signal units; less field/combat-deployment-driven than most Army MOS, though soldiers remain deployable. General characterization, not independently sourced to a specific DoD tempo study this session.
deployment_and_lifestyle.family_considerations: Frequently more CONUS-stable duty stations than combat-arms MOS given the fixed-facility nature of cyber operations centers, though PCS moves and TDY still occur. General characterization, not independently sourced this session.

entry_requirements.asvab_line_score: No single Navy 'line score' - Navy ratings qualify via ASVAB subtest-sum combinations, consistent with interest-taxonomy.json's note that Navy has no line-score system. For CWT: AR + 2(MK) + GS >= 255, OR VE + AR + MK + MC >= 235, OR CT + MK + VE >= 173 AND CT >= 60 (CT = Cyber Test, a supplemental Navy aptitude test beyond the standard ASVAB). Search-sourced (ratemyasvab.com); not independently confirmed against a primary Navy COOL/NAVET document this session.

This is a hard gate independent of ASVAB score: per SEAD 4 (the government-wide National Security Adjudicative Guidelines), common disqualifiers include foreign influence/preference (close foreign-national contacts, dual citizenship, foreign-government ties), financial considerations (unresolved delinquent debt, bankruptcy pattern, tax delinquency), and drug involvement/substance misuse (recent illegal drug use or a pattern of misuse) - a qualifying ASVAB/CT score does not offset a disqualifying finding under these guidelines.

civilian_crosswalk.source: BLS Occupational Outlook Handbook, Information Security Analysts, median $124,910/yr, range $69,660-$186,420, May 2024 data. O*NET code is moderate-confidence trained knowledge, not re-fetched this session.

veteran_benefits.gi_bill_tier_note: Post-9/11 GI Bill (Ch. 33) eligibility scales with cumulative qualifying active-duty days: 100% at 36+ months; 90% at 30-35; 80% at 24-29; 70% at 18-23; 60% at 6-17; 50% at 90 days-5 months. No tier below 50%.
veteran_benefits.gi_bill_transferability_note: Transferable to spouse/dependent children (up to 36 months combined) only while still serving; requires 6+ years of service and a new 4-year additional service obligation.
veteran_benefits.va_home_loan_note: Full-entitlement veterans face no VA-imposed loan limit; no down payment or PMI required at/below appraised value. Funding fee (1.25%-3.3%) applies unless the veteran draws VA disability compensation.
veteran_benefits.va_healthcare_priority_group_note: Priority Group (1-8) depends on VA disability rating, combat-service era, and income; Groups 1-3 generally owe no copays.
veteran_benefits.state_benefit_variance_note: State veteran benefits are set by individual state legislatures and are NOT a single national figure - verify by state of legal residence.

education_benefits.gi_bill_summary: Post-9/11 GI Bill (Ch. 33): full net tuition/fees with no cap at public schools; up to $29,920.95/yr at private/foreign schools (2025-2026 AY); MHA keyed to school ZIP; $1,000/yr book stipend.
education_benefits.tuition_assistance: DoD-wide standard $250/semester-hour, $4,500/fiscal-year cap, administered through the Air Force Virtual Education Center (AFVEC). An 18-semester-hour/fiscal-year usage limit is reported but UNVERIFIED at primary-source level.
education_benefits.credentialing_assistance: Air Force Credentialing Assistance (via AFVEC): reported cap of up to $4,500/year. Search-sourced; not independently fetched from cool.osd.mil this session (fetch attempt failed with a certificate error).
education_benefits.coop_or_apprenticeship_notes: No 1B4X1-specific apprenticeship/co-op program was independently identified this session; standard AFVEC Credentialing Assistance funding applies.

deployment_and_lifestyle.typical_tempo: Garrison-based work at fixed cyber squadrons (e.g., Keesler AFB, JBSA-adjacent units); AFSC 1B4X1 supports Air Combat Command's cyber mission, and some assignments (cyber protection teams) do deploy in direct support of combatant commands. General characterization, not independently sourced this session.
deployment_and_lifestyle.family_considerations: Relatively more CONUS-stable duty stations than flying/combat AFSCs given the fixed-facility nature of cyber squadrons, though PCS moves and TDY still occur. General characterization, not independently sourced this session.

entry_requirements.asvab_line_score: Marine Corps Cyber Test (CT) score >= 55 - a supplemental Marine Corps aptitude test, distinct from the standard ASVAB GT/EL/MM line scores. Search-sourced; not independently fetched to primary-source confidence this session (information.marines.mil returned HTTP 403 on direct fetch; cool.osd.mil's 1721 page returned a certificate error on direct fetch).

entry_requirements.security_clearance: SCI eligibility via a Single Scope Background Investigation (Tier 5); requires an SCI Pre-Screen Interview with the HQMC Special Security Officer and willingness to submit to a counterintelligence-scope polygraph examination. Search-sourced, citing official Marine Corps guidance (marines.mil); not independently fetched to primary-source confidence this session. This is a hard gate independent of the Cyber Test score: per SEAD 4 (the government-wide National Security Adjudicative Guidelines), common disqualifiers include foreign influence/preference (close foreign-national contacts, dual citizenship, foreign-government ties), financial considerations (unresolved delinquent debt, bankruptcy pattern, tax delinquency), and drug involvement/substance misuse (recent illegal drug use or a pattern of misuse) - a qualifying aptitude-test score does not offset a disqualifying finding under these guidelines.
entry_requirements.other: U.S. citizenship required. Correction to this project's interest-taxonomy.json: MOS '1710' is NOT an enlisted 'Network Administrator' role as the taxonomy's example listed it - 1710 is 'Offensive Cyberspace Warfare Officer,' an officer/warrant-officer-only MOS (see career_progression.officer_conversion_path). 1721 (this record) is the enlisted Cyberspace Warfare Operator MOS and is the correct enlisted entry point in this occupational field.

retirement.twenty_year_cliff_note: The defined-benefit pension is all-or-nothing at 20 years of service; separating one day short yields $0 pension. TSP savings are the only guaranteed retirement money before 20 years and roll into a civilian 401(k)/IRA on separation.
retirement.continuation_pay_note: Statutory window is service years 8-12 (Army has published a year-7 start; whether the Marine Corps has adopted an earlier window was not independently verified this session). Statutory multiplier range is 2.5x-13x monthly basic pay for active-component members, in exchange for a minimum 3-year additional service obligation; the exact Marine Corps multiplier was not independently confirmed this session.
retirement.guard_reserve_points_note: NOT interchangeable with active-duty retirement. Marine Corps Reserve uses a points system (1 pt/drill period, 1 pt/active-duty day, up to 15 membership points/year) toward a 20-'good year' threshold, with pension starting at age 60, reducible to as low as 50 under a FY2008 NDAA active-duty-mobilization credit.
retirement.lump_sum_option_note: At 20+ years, a retiree may elect a 25% or 50% lump-sum of the discounted present value of future retired pay, with correspondingly reduced monthly pay until Social Security Full Retirement Age (~67).

transition.skillbridge_notes: DoD-wide SkillBridge: up to 180 days of permissive duty during the final 180 days on active duty. Unit commander approval is the real gate and can be withheld for mission/staffing reasons.
transition.tap_program_summary: Mandatory DoD-wide Transition Assistance Program; must begin no later than 365 days before separation, culminating in a Capstone review of Career Readiness Standards.
transition.dd214_significance: Governs eligibility for VA disability compensation, VA healthcare, GI Bill/education benefits, VA home loan COE, federal hiring preference, and state veteran benefits. Block 24 (character of service) gates most benefits.
va_disability_common_claims: Musculoskeletal strain from sustained console/watch-floor work - reasoned inference, not drawn from a cyber-specific DoD/VA claims dataset.
Sleep-disorder/circadian conditions from rotating watch schedules - same inference caveat.
Mental health conditions - same inference caveat.

Standard Marine Corps entry-training musculoskeletal conditions (from Recruit Training/MCT, which this MOS shares with every non-infantry Marine) are plausible but not specialty-differentiating and are not listed as a 1721-specific claim.
PACT Act presumptive conditions if deployed to a covered theater - broad category, not cyber-specific, per data/transition.json.
transition.vr_e_eligibility_note: Chapter 31 (VR&E) requires a 20%+ service-connected disability rating creating an employment handicap; funds tuition/training plus a monthly subsistence allowance (FY2026 max $3,439.23/month) from an entitlement bucket separate from the GI Bill.

TSP savings are the only guaranteed retirement money before 20 years and roll into a civilian 401(k)/IRA on separation. The Coast Guard participates in the same military retirement system (BRS) as the DoD branches by statute despite being a DHS component - not independently re-confirmed as Coast Guard-specific this session.
retirement.continuation_pay_note: Statutory window is service years 8-12 (Army has published a year-7 start; whether the Coast Guard has adopted an earlier window was not independently verified this session). Statutory multiplier range is 2.5x-13x monthly basic pay for active-component members, in exchange for a minimum 3-year additional service obligation.
retirement.guard_reserve_points_note: NOT interchangeable with active-duty retirement. Coast Guard Reserve uses the same general points system as the other services (1 pt/drill period, 1 pt/active-duty day, up to 15 membership points/year) toward a 20-'good year' threshold, with pension starting at age 60 - not independently re-confirmed as Coast-Guard-specific this session.
retirement.lump_sum_option_note: At 20+ years, a retiree may elect a 25% or 50% lump-sum of the discounted present value of future retired pay, with correspondingly reduced monthly pay until Social Security Full Retirement Age (~67).

transition.skillbridge_notes: DoD-wide SkillBridge policy was applied in this project's TRANSITION research; Coast-Guard-specific variances in SkillBridge/TAP administration (DHS component, not DoD) were NOT independently verified and may differ. Up to 180 days of permissive duty during the final 180 days on active duty is the general rule; unit commander approval is the real gate.
transition.tap_program_summary: Mandatory DoD-wide-modeled Transition Assistance Program; must begin no later than 365 days before separation, culminating in a Capstone review of Career Readiness Standards. Coast-Guard-specific administration was not independently verified this session.
transition.dd214_significance: Governs eligibility for VA disability compensation, VA healthcare, GI Bill/education benefits, VA home loan COE, federal hiring preference, and state veteran benefits. Block 24 (character of service) gates most benefits.
va_disability_common_claims: Musculoskeletal strain from sustained console/watch-floor and shipboard IT-support work - reasoned inference, not drawn from a cyber-specific DoD/VA claims dataset.
Hearing-related conditions if regularly working near engineering/machinery spaces aboard cutters - reasoned inference.
Mental health conditions - reasoned inference.
PACT Act presumptive conditions if deployed to a covered theater - broad category, not cyber-specific, per data/transition.json.
transition.vr_e_eligibility_note: Chapter 31 (VR&E) requires a 20%+ service-connected disability rating creating an employment handicap; funds tuition/training plus a monthly subsistence allowance (FY2026 max $3,439.23/month) from an entitlement bucket separate from the GI Bill.
transition.source: data/transition.json (this project's TRANSITION output, including its own Coast-Guard-variance caveat)

civilian_crosswalk.credential_gap_note: Shipboard/shore IT-administration experience does not by itself confer a civilian certification. CCNA, Network+, and similar credentials require separately scheduling and passing a proctored exam regardless of demonstrated operational skill. The military does not hand you the certification automatically: Coast Guard COOL (see education_benefits.credentialing_assistance above) funds the exam fee while still serving, but the member must still schedule, sit, and pass the exam independently.
civilian_crosswalk.source: BLS Occupational Outlook Handbook, Network and Computer Systems Administrators, median $96,800/yr, 10th-90th percentile range $60,320-$150,320, May 2024 data. O*NET code is moderate-confidence trained knowledge, not re-fetched this session.

veteran_benefits.gi_bill_tier_note: Post-9/11 GI Bill (Ch. 33) eligibility scales with cumulative qualifying active-duty days: 100% at 36+ months; 90% at 30-35; 80% at 24-29; 70% at 18-23; 60% at 6-17; 50% at 90 days-5 months. No tier below 50%. GI Bill eligibility rules are federal (VA-administered) and apply the same way to Coast Guard service as to the DoD branches.
veteran_benefits.gi_bill_transferability_note: Transferable to spouse/dependent children (up to 36 months combined) only while still serving; requires 6+ years of service and a new 4-year additional service obligation.
veteran_benefits.va_home_loan_note: Full-entitlement veterans face no VA-imposed loan limit; no down payment or PMI required at/below appraised value. Funding fee (1.25%-3.3%) applies unless the veteran draws VA disability compensation.
veteran_benefits.va_healthcare_priority_group_note: Priority Group (1-8) depends on VA disability rating, combat-service era, and income; Groups 1-3 generally owe no copays.
veteran_benefits.state_benefit_variance_note: State veteran benefits are set by individual state legislatures and are NOT a single national figure - verify by state of legal residence.

education_benefits.gi_bill_summary: Post-9/11 GI Bill (Ch. 33): full net tuition/fees with no cap at public schools; up to $29,920.95/yr at private/foreign schools (2025-2026 AY); MHA keyed to school ZIP; $1,000/yr book stipend.
education_benefits.tuition_assistance: Reported to follow the same $250/semester-hour, $4,500/fiscal-year DoD-wide caps, administered under DHS via the Coast Guard Institute rather than DoD. Search-sourced only per data/education-benefits.json, possibly conflated with the Credentialing Assistance figure by the search summarizer that produced it - confirm at gocoastguard.com/uscg.mil before publishing.
education_benefits.credentialing_assistance: UNVERIFIED. Coast Guard Credentialing Opportunities On-Line (COOL) exists, but a specific dollar cap was not confirmed this session or in this project's EDU-BENEFITS pass (cool.osd.mil fetch failed with a certificate error).
education_benefits.coop_or_apprenticeship_notes: No IT-specific apprenticeship/co-op program was independently identified this session.

deployment_and_lifestyle.typical_tempo: ITs serve aboard cutters supporting tactical command/control/communications systems, and ashore at Electronics Support Units; tempo is tied to cutter patrol schedules rather than combat-deployment cycles. Source: gocoastguard.com (search-sourced).
deployment_and_lifestyle.family_considerations: Coast Guard assignments are predominantly CONUS (including Alaska, Hawaii, and U.S. territories) with fewer traditional combat-zone deployments than the DoD branches; cutter-based ITs may see periodic patrol-length family separations. General characterization, not independently sourced this session.

Every civilian_crosswalk block names a credential_gap_note so seamless credential transfer is never implied. The extra calls were spent specifically on: (a) discovering and correcting the Navy CTN-to-CWT rating change and the Marine Corps 1710-vs-1721 mislabeling inherited from interest-taxonomy.json, (b) sourcing recruit-training and technical-school lengths to primary-adjacent confidence rather than guessing, (c) sourcing BLS civilian salary data required by the schema's estimated_civilian_salary_range_usd field, and (d) attempting (unsuccessfully, and reported as such) to confirm a Space Force numeric SFSC.
